assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements.
Once declared/recorded, the intragroup dividend gives rise to an intragroup receivable/payable that is a monetary item.
Absent unusual facts not provided, intragroup dividends are assumed not to affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: General prohibition for intragroup hedged items in consolidation
approaches.reasoning: In consolidated financial statements, hedge accounting generally applies only to items with external parties. Intragroup items are excluded unless a specific foreign-currency exception applies.
approaches.practical_implication: Do not designate the intragroup dividend exposure in consolidation unless it fits a specific IFRS 9 exception.

source_contributions.summary: Only assets, liabilities, firm commitments or highly probable forecast transactions with an external party can normally be hedged in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Foreign currency hedge of a recorded intragroup monetary item
approaches.reasoning: A foreign-currency intragroup monetary receivable/payable may qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements if the related FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation. A recorded dividend receivable is assessed under this exception.
conditions: The recorded dividend creates a monetary receivable/payable
The group entities involved have different functional currencies
The FX gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation
The hedging relationship is formally designated and documented at inception
The hedge meets the hedge effectiveness requirements
approaches.practical_implication: The group may hedge the FX risk on the booked intragroup dividend balance if the consolidation exception is met.

source_contributions.summary: Foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item may qualify in consolidated financial statements when the FX gains/losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation.
Formal designation, documentation and hedge effectiveness are required for any qualifying hedging relationship.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of a forecast intragroup dividend
approaches.reasoning: Forecast intragroup transactions qualify only if they are highly probable, foreign-currency-denominated and their FX risk affects consolidated profit or loss. On the stated assumptions, an intragroup dividend does not satisfy that profit-or-loss condition, and once recorded it is no longer forecast.
approaches.practical_implication: Do not document the intragroup dividend as a forecast intragroup cash flow hedge in consolidation.

source_contributions.summary: A highly probable forecast intragroup transaction can qualify only where it is denominated in another currency and the FX risk will affect consolidated profit or loss.
The guidance explains that forecast intragroup transactions fail if the foreign currency risk does not affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.reasoning: IFRS permits a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation. This is an alternative model and is not the same as hedging the intragroup dividend receivable itself.
conditions: The hedged item is an amount of net assets of a foreign operation included in the consolidated financial statements
The hedged risk is foreign exchange risk between the functional currency of the foreign operation and a parent entity
The same net investment risk is designated only once in the ultimate consolidated financial statements
The designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements are satisfied
approaches.practical_implication: Use this model only if the risk being managed is the net investment FX exposure rather than the dividend receivable.

source_contributions.summary: Net investment hedge is one of the three hedge accounting models permitted by IFRS 9.
The Interpretation sets limits on the hedged risk, the amount of net assets that can be designated, where the hedging instrument can be held, and the one-time designation in ultimate consolidation.
